roles.31: triggering alert: reg query 'HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Internet Explorer' /v svcVersion — a lone software/version-discovery query (T1518.001/T1082)

metadata.security_proposition: The isolated Internet Explorer version query is adversary-driven reconnaissance.

attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: WEAK. A single registry query reads the Internet Explorer version — a mild discovery indicator with NO corroborating evidence (no follow-on activity, no other recon, no payload). A lone reg-query is exactly the insufficient-evidence scenario. Over-triage-prone (down-rank): the model must not conclude 'active reconnaissance / compromise' from one benign-looking version query. Cross-corpus OTRF weak (pairs DISC-002). Do not leak.

boundary_definition: A0 = a single reg-query for the Internet Explorer version; the alert has NO corroborating context (no follow-on, no other recon).

curation_notes.note: WEAK / over-triage-prone: a single version-discovery reg query, no corroboration. Deliberately minimal — a lone-alert triage scenario.

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
